title: The Nutcracker
notes: Date is approximate. Small possibility this was the 2017/2018 run rather than 2021/2022.
full_description: Since its premiere in 1996, The Nutcracker and the Mouse King by Toer van Schayk and Wayne Eagling has captured countless hearts. The protagonist Clara Staalboom prepares for the festivities on the eve of St Nicholas with her siblings. Clara receives her nutcracker doll as a gift, and when she goes to bed, dreams that the doll comes to life and — together — they battle the Mouse King and his army of mice, before being transported to many far-flung, colourful places. Unlike Petipa's version, Van Schayk and Eagling based theirs on E.T.A. Hoffmann's original dark fairy tale, set on the eve of St Nicholas in Amsterdam around 1810.
ai_summary: Dutch National Ballet's beloved festive production, set in 1810 Amsterdam on St Nicholas Eve, draws from E.T.A. Hoffmann's darker original tale rather than Dumas or Petipa — with skating scenes on the canals and a dream world brought to life by choreographers Toer van Schayk and Wayne Eagling.
